elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_0
elements.text: Mediterranean（地中海的）countries about 6,000 years ago.可知橄榄树最初可能生长在亚洲，故选A。 80.D 细节理解题。在解题是注意排除法的运用，根据短文可知橄榄油在古代就用于做饭，建造船只，也当 做药用，但是没有提及橄榄油是怎么来的。故选D。 81.B细节理解题。根据 In ancient Greece, for example, anyone who cut down an olive tree was heavily punished. 故选B。 82.D 归纳理解题。根据短文主要描述了橄榄树的发展，橄榄油的用途。故选D。 考点：科普类短文阅读。

mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_2
elements.text: While many young people were enjoying the summer vacation, Zach Bonner was working his hardest. Zach started walking from Valrico, Florida, his hometown, on Christmas, 2009. He reached Los Angeles nine months later in September, 2010. He covered a total of 2,478 miles and raised $120,00 for kids in need. Along the way, Zach atended school online. His mother, brother and sister took turns to walk or drive together with him.

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_3
elements.text: Although he is very young, Zach has a long history of helping others. When a terrible storm hit town in 2004, Zach, when six, pulled a wagon（小推车） through his community and collected food for people in need.

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_4
elements.text: He has raised $400,0 for his Litle Red Wagon Foundation since then. It gives money to projects which help homeless children. In 2007, Zach began walking to support a children's charity（慈善组织） in Tampa, Florida. He finished his journey 23 days later, 280 miles away in Tallahassee. Then in the summer of 2009, he trekked about 670 miles from Atlanta to Washington, D.C., in just two months.

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_5
elements.text: "As long as there are homeless kids, I will never stop walking for them."Zach says.

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_6
elements.text: 83. Wha t did Zach do during the summer vacation in 2010?

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_7
elements.text: A. He stayed at home to look after his family. B. He travelled to his hometown with his family. C.. He joined in a school activity with other kids. D. He walked to collect money for kids in need.

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_8
elements.text: 84. We can learn from Paragraph 2 that Zach

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_9
elements.text: A. began to help people at a very early age B. made money to pay for his education C. enjoyed playing around in his community D. worked very hard for his family

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_10
elements.text: 85. At what age did Zach start walking to support a Tampa children's charity?

elements.id: mineru_exam_paper_2016年安徽省中考英语试题（解析）_page_015_11
elements.text: A. At six. = B. At nine. C. At eleven. D. At twelve.
